meaning: n.寄膳者,寄膳宿者,寄宿生
examples: On my first time I accidentally crashed into a snow boarder.
The bomb struck a security convoy, not far from the Afghan boarder.
Standing on the floor, a young boarder named Carl was admiring the view.
Mr Lascelles eventually helped the body boarder to scramble to safety over the rocks.
He admitted this might be far away and that Edward might need to be a boarder.
